description: Automated hotel report processing system. Receives daily report emails, parses and transforms the data using GL account mappings, and delivers JE and StatJE CSV reports to accounting.

external.name: Visual Matrix Hotels
external.description: 11 properties sending daily PDF reports via email
external.details: Sends one email per property per day with a PDF attachment (Daily Report)

external.name: IHG / Opera Hotels
external.details: Sends two .txt files per day: trial_balance_*.txt and stat_dmy_seg_*.txt from hsaor-donotreply@hotels.ihg.com

external.name: Choice Hotels
external.description: 3 properties (Comfort Inn Missoula MT118, Comfort Inn & Suites Ashland OR258, Comfort Inn & Suites Spokane Valley WA244)
external.details: Sends two CSV files per day wrapped in a ZIP attachment from AUTO_MAIL_DELIVERY_SYSTEM@choicehotels.com. Files: Hotel Statistics_*.csv and Hotel Journal Summary_*.csv. Property code is embedded in ZIP filename (e.g. All_Night_Audit_Reports_WA244_HOTEL STATS_2026-06-23.zip). Implementation complete in feature/choice-hotels-pipeline — pending SSM configuration and deployment.

external.name: NetSuite
external.description: Accounting system — final destination for JE and StatJE reports
external.details: Reports are imported manually or via automation from the emailed CSV files

external.name: Accounting Recipients
external.description: Per-property email addresses that receive the daily reports
external.details: Configured per property in property-config.ts. In production, override-email SSM parameter is NOT set so emails go to real recipients.

aws_services.name: SES Receipt Rules
aws_services.details: Receipt rule stores raw email to incoming-files S3 bucket and triggers email-processor Lambda

aws_services.name: SES Send
aws_services.description: Sends processed report emails to accounting recipients
aws_services.details: Called by file-processor Lambda after generating JE/StatJE reports

aws_services.name: S3: incoming-files
aws_services.description: Stores raw email attachments received from hotels

aws_services.name: S3: processed-files
aws_services.description: Stores generated JE and StatJE CSV reports and processed markers

aws_services.name: S3: mapping-files
aws_services.description: Stores Excel mapping workbooks for GL account transformation
aws_services.structure: VisualMatrix XLSX at root level; Opera XLSX under opera/ prefix; Choice Hotels XLSX under choice/ prefix

aws_services.name: Lambda: email-processor
aws_services.description: Triggered by SES. Parses incoming email, extracts attachments, stores in S3.
responsibilities: Identify property from sender email via SSM email-mapping parameter
Store attachments to S3 incoming-files bucket with unique UUID filename
Handles duplicate file prevention via UUID in filename
Extracts CSV files from ZIP attachments (Choice Hotels); routes via __choice__ sentinel + choice:{code} SSM lookup

aws_services.name: Lambda: file-processor
aws_services.description: Triggered by EventBridge daily at 1 PM MST. Processes all files from the last 24 hours.
responsibilities: Discover all files in S3 incoming-files from last 24 hours
Check for duplicates via S3 processed-markers
Route to Visual Matrix, Opera, or Choice Hotels pipeline based on filename pattern
Load appropriate mapping file from S3
Parse, transform, and generate JE and StatJE CSV reports
Send reports via SES to property recipients

aws_services.name: EventBridge Schedule
aws_services.description: Triggers file-processor Lambda daily at 1 PM MST (20:00 UTC)

parameters.description: JSON object mapping sender email address to property slug
If set, all outbound reports are redirected to this address. NOT set in production.
The email address SES receives mail on
The from address used when sending reports

aws_services.name: SQS Dead Letter Queue
aws_services.description: Captures failed Lambda invocations for investigation
aws_services.details: Both email-processor and file-processor have DLQs. DLQ messages trigger SNS alert.

aws_services.name: SNS: Alerts
aws_services.description: Sends alert emails when Lambda failures land in DLQ
aws_services.details: Subscribed to CloudWatch alarms on DLQ depth

aws_services.name: CloudWatch
aws_services.description: Logs, alarms, and monitoring for Lambda functions
aws_services.details: Lambda logs streamed to CloudWatch log groups. Alarms on DLQ depth, Lambda errors, and duration.

code_modules.name: FileProcessor
code_modules.description: Entry point and orchestrator for file-processor Lambda. Routes to VM or Opera pipeline.

code_modules.name: PropertyConfigService
code_modules.description: All 12 property configurations including NetSuite IDs, recipient emails, and roomsAvailable for Opera properties

code_modules.name: PDFParser
code_modules.description: Parses Visual Matrix PDF reports using pdf-parse v1 with custom pagerender callback for pipe-delimited column detection

code_modules.name: VisualMatrixParser
code_modules.description: Parses pipe-delimited text extracted from Visual Matrix PDFs into structured account line data

code_modules.name: VisualMatrixMappingLoader
code_modules.description: Loads Excel mapping workbook from root of S3 mapping bucket (non-opera/ prefix only)

code_modules.name: OperaTrialBalanceParser
code_modules.description: Parses trial_balance_*.txt Opera export. Extracts transaction rows and CS_ summary block entries.

code_modules.name: OperaStatParser
code_modules.description: Parses stat_dmy_seg_*.txt Opera export. Extracts room occupancy and segment data.

code_modules.name: OperaMappingLoader
code_modules.description: Loads Opera XLSX mapping from opera/ S3 prefix. Supports dual mapping: Map<TRX_CODE, OperaMappingEntry[]>.

code_modules.name: OperaTransformation
code_modules.description: Transforms Opera parsed data into JE and StatJE records. Handles ADR/Occupancy/RevPAR, dual mapping, and Visa/MC/Discover combining.

code_modules.name: JournalEntryGenerator
code_modules.description: Generates JE CSV output with debit/credit logic, subsidiary, and location from property config

code_modules.name: DuplicateDetector
code_modules.description: Checks S3 processed-markers to skip already-processed property+date combinations

pipelines.name: Visual Matrix (PDF) Pipeline
pipelines.triggerPattern: Any email attachment that is NOT named trial_balance_* or stat_dmy_seg_*
steps: Hotel sends email with PDF attachment
SES receipts email → stores raw to S3 incoming-files → triggers email-processor Lambda
email-processor extracts PDF, looks up property slug from SSM email-mapping, stores to S3 daily-files/{slug}/
EventBridge triggers file-processor at 1 PM MST
file-processor discovers all files from last 24h, groups by property
DuplicateDetector checks S3 processed-markers — skips if already processed
PDFParser extracts text using pdf-parse with custom pagerender (pipe-delimited columns)
VisualMatrixParser parses pipe-delimited lines into account line data
Excel mapping loaded from S3 mapping-files root (excludes opera/ prefix)
Transformation engine applies GL account mappings to produce JE lines
JE and StatJE CSV files written to S3 processed-files
Processed marker written to S3 processed-markers
SES sends JE+StatJE as email attachments to property recipients

pipelines.name: Opera / IHG Pipeline
pipelines.triggerPattern: Files named trial_balance_*.txt AND stat_dmy_seg_*.txt for same property+date
steps: IHG sends email with two .txt attachments from hsaor-donotreply@hotels.ihg.com
SES receipts → email-processor stores both files to S3 daily-files/holiday-inn-express-clover-lane/
EventBridge triggers file-processor at 1 PM MST
file-processor detects Opera files by filename pattern, pairs trial_balance + stat files
DuplicateDetector checks processed-markers
OperaTrialBalanceParser parses transaction rows and CS_ summary block
OperaStatParser parses segment rows and totalRoomsOccupied
OperaMappingLoader loads latest XLSX from S3 mapping-files/opera/ prefix
OperaTransformation builds JE lines (including dual-mapping, Visa/MC/Discover combining, summary-block accounts)
OperaTransformation builds StatJE lines (including ADR, Occupancy, RevPAR calculations)
JE and StatJE CSV files written to S3 processed-files
Processed marker written
SES sends reports to property recipients

pipelines.name: Choice Hotels Pipeline
pipelines.triggerPattern: Files named Hotel Statistics_*.csv AND Hotel Journal Summary_*.csv for same property+date (extracted from ZIP)
steps: Choice Hotels sends one email per property per day from AUTO_MAIL_DELIVERY_SYSTEM@choicehotels.com with a ZIP attachment
ZIP filename contains property code, e.g. All_Night_Audit_Reports_WA244_HOTEL STATS_2026-06-23.zip
SES receipts → email-processor sees sender mapped to __choice__ sentinel
email-processor extracts property code from ZIP filename, resolves slug via choice:{code} SSM lookup
adm-zip extracts CSV files from ZIP; each stored individually under daily-files/{propertySlug}/
EventBridge triggers file-processor at 1 PM MST
file-processor detects Choice files by filename pattern, pairs hotel-statistics + journal-summary files
Raw CSV content bypasses csv-parser and is fed directly to ChoiceJournalSummaryParser / ChoiceHotelStatsParser
ChoiceMappingLoader loads latest XLSX from S3 mapping-files/choice/ prefix
transformJournalSummaryToJERecords builds JE lines (Transaction Code + ledger column sums)
transformHotelStatsToStatJERecords builds StatJE lines (column header matching, RevPAR date resolution, 3 trailing zero rows)
JE and StatJE CSV files written to S3 processed-files
SES sends reports to property recipients
